characters.name: Michael Adams
characters.description: "It's amazing how much you can misssomebody you never met." Michael Adamsis a married man and expectant father, and he's also the son of deceased military sergeantPaul Adams. He is portrayed byBalthazar Getty.

characters.name: Paul Adams
characters.description: "When that baby comes intohislife...man, don't miss a second. Not one second. Nothing waits for you. That's the one thing he's gotta remember." Paul Adams (Wentworth Miller)is a military sergeant who fought during the Vietnam War in Fort Driscoll and died in action. Sergeant Paul Adams was an expert marksman sniper in the fifth infantry and won a bronze star and a purple heart for his services. He was married and conceived a son with his wife, and the family lived together in Grandview. His wife told their son that he was "funny and reliable, and he had very neat handwriting" along with other stories so he "knew he was a hero". He had to be in Vietnam while his wife was awaiting their baby, and he constantly sent letters to her, his last one being to set a picture of him next to and facing his son's crib. In June of 1972, he and several other soldiers were dispatched in a helicopter during a mission. Just as they were passing over Lo Valley, the helicopter took heavy fire, and eventually it fell. After so much damage, Adams' last sight before he died is a waterfall in the valley, and he was happy the last thing he saw was so beautiful. Just before the helicopter crashed and everyone on board and anyone else below and/or nearby died, he cried over the thought of never seeing his and his wife's newborn child, whom his wife gave birth to three months after. His wife named their childMichaelafter Adams' father, the named Adams wanted for him. For years he was never found and was classified "Missing in Action". After Michael grew up, Paul's wife passed away, and she went into the light.

characters.name: Vera Adams
characters.description: "Sweetie, just call the Pentagon. See what they know. Maybe she was right, maybe you'll find something. Maybe you need this. It'll bring you the closure you've always wanted." Vera Adamsis the wife ofMichael Adamsand the mother of their son. She is portrayed by actressAllison McDonell, who has few credits and has not be very widely recognized.

characters.name: Andrea Marino
characters.description: Andrea MarinoBiographical InformationBornSeptember 18, 1970DiedMay 11, 2006 (aged 35)April 28, 2006(according to "The Book of Changes")StatusDeceasedCrossed OverAliasAndrea Joyce MarinoFamilial InformationChildrenNoneSiblingsMitch Marino(brother)ParentsUnnamed father †Professional InformationProfessionMelinda'spartner atSame As It Never WasAssistant District Attorney in New York (formerly)WorkplaceSame As It Never Was AntiquesAppearancesFirstPilotLastLove Never DiesCount23Seasons1Portrayed byAisha Tyler Andrea Joyce Marinoworked in New York, as a lawyer, until she moved toGrandview. She then obtained a job atThe Same As It Never Was, the antique store owned byMelinda Gordon. When Andrea first moved to Grandview, she was a little skeptical of Melinda. When Melinda told Andrea of her special gift that she sees Earth-bound spirits, Melinda at first, thought Andrea would reject her. But instead, she accepted Melinda's gift and they become best friends. Melinda told Andrea her ability when Andrea was being haunted by a ghost that used to live in her apartment. Andrea was always open-minded and insightful and she tried to be compassionate but also assertive regarding what she stands for, evident of when she got a con man to return the money he stole from her and Melinda[1]and also when she recounted a bigoted security guard tackling her to ground because he thought she stole a bottle of wine.[2]In the season 1 finale, and season 2 premiere, Andrea was on her way to her brother Mitch's apartment in the city, but her car was in the path of the plane when it crashed. Andrea did not realize she was dead untilThe One (Part Two)when Mitch finally arrives at the store to meet with Melinda, he runs right past Andrea as if she wasn't there, but it was only when Melinda hugged Mitch and offered her condolences that Andrea finally understood that she had died in the crash, not Mitch. Melinda tried to tell Andrea but the latter would always interrupt. While Melinda tried to figure out how to help her best friend cross over, she had to contend withRomano, a former cult leader who died by leading a mass suicide in 1939. He wanted to take Andrea for himself and convince her to avoid the light and remain earth bound based on lies and false promises. In the end, she made peace with Mitch and crossed over intothe Light.

characters.name: Becca Cahill
characters.description: Becca CahillBiographical InformationStatusAliveFamilial InformationSiblings?ParentsTony Cahill(father)Joan Cahill(mother)Professional InformationProfessionYoung Ghost whispererAppearancesFirstAll Ghosts Lead to GrandviewLastAll Ghosts Lead to GrandviewSeasons3Portrayed byMakenzie Vega Becca Cahillis a young girl that can see spirits. Becca has the ability to speak to, and see, the souls of the people who have died. She can see, hear and speak to the deceased.

characters.name: Beth Gordon
characters.description: Beth GordonAdultYoungBiographical InformationBorn1940sStatusAliveFamilial InformationMaritalThomas Gordon†(Ex-Husband)Paul Eastman†(Ex-Fiance)ChildrenMelinda Gordon(Daughter)ParentsMary Ann Patterson†(Mother)OthersTessa Lucas†(Great-Great-Grandmother)Julia Lee Lucas†(Great-Grandmother)Aiden Lucas(Grandson)Jim Clancy†(Son-in-law)Sam Lucas(Son-in-law)AppearancesFirstMelinda's First GhostLastPater FamiliasCount4Seasons1,2,3Portrayed byAnne Archer Paul Eastman†(Ex-Fiance) Beth Gordon isMelinda's mother andJim's mother-in-law. She is one of the only known members of Melinda's family to not inherit the ability to see ghosts. It is not known why the ability "skipped" Beth. This fact was later proven false in the episode "Melinda's First Ghost" (Season 1, Episode 15) where Melinda and Beth have an argument about Melinda's friend, Sarah, from the past. Melinda finds out Beth is lying after Beth mistakenly quotes the ghost of Sarah; she thought Melinda has said it. Therefore is is proven that Beth can indeed see ghosts but has repressed her gift because she finds it a burden. Jim tells her at one point that his wife runs towards her truth/gift whereas Beth runs away from it.

characters.name: Carl Sessick
characters.description: Carl the watcher Carl Sessickonce worked at a funeral home and he was the one that dumped the bodies in the lake. The ghosts of the people he dumped in the lake were haunting him before he died. He appeared in "Body of Water." In the end, he joined theWatchers. In "Book of Changes", he broke the rules by interfering instead of just warning.  He made someone steal the Book of Changes from the library at Rockland University and made him hide it in Zoe's house. He toldMelindathatAidencan do much more than her.  He also told her that Aiden is an empath and is connected to her. He is potrayed by David Clennon

characters.name: Delia Banks
characters.description: Delia BanksBiographical InformationBornc. 1961 (age 64)StatusAliveFamilial InformationMaritalCharlie Banks†(husband)Tim Flaherty(dated briefly)ChildrenNed BanksProfessional InformationProfessionReal Estate AgentAntique Shop Employee & Partner (formerly)WorkplaceBanks Cutler Real EstateSun Briar Realty (formerly)Same As It Never Was (formerly)AppearancesFirstLove Still Won't DieLastThe Children's ParadePortrayed byCamryn ManheimDelia Bankslost her husband and the father of her teenage son three years prior to her appearance on the show. Delia became a real estate agent after Charlie's death. Tim Flaherty(dated briefly) Delia meetsMelinda Gordonafter Melinda calls Delia about her son Ned having tried to shoplift from Melinda's antique store. Delia tells Melinda about how stressful her job as a real estate agent is, so Melinda offers her a job to help work at the antique store, which Delia accepts. They begin a friendship, though Delia does not know about Melinda's gift at first. In the episode "Delia's First Ghost" Melinda has to tell Delia about her gift because Charlie's spirit is haunting her. At first Delia calls Melinda crazy, admitting that she had seen Melinda talking to herself and was worried. Delia even goes as far as to tell Melinda not to expect her at work again. By the end of the episode Delia learns, with Melinda's help, that Charlie is only haunting her because he wants her to be safe. While still a little skeptical of Melinda's gift, Delia tells her that she will open the shop the next morning. During her high school life, Delia was in a drama club, and had become good friends with a certain girl. At the time she was known for her recklessness and her bouncing off the walls nature. One of her closest friends died (Angie Holsenbach) and she was utterly changed into what she is now.

characters.name: Dylan Cho
characters.description: Dylan ChoBiographical InformationStatusAliveFamilial InformationParentsSharon Cho(mother)AppearancesFirstThe CrossingLastMelinda's First GhostCount3Portrayed byJordan Dang Dylan Chois a young boy who lives with his mother,Sharon, inGrandview. While his mother is working in the town'shair salon, Dylan is left to his own devices. He doesn't like the way the salon smells, and often walks around town,noticeably lonely.

characters.name: Eli James
characters.description: Eli JamesBiographical InformationStatusAliveFamilial InformationParentsRay James †(father)Evelyn James †(mother)OthersZoe Ramos †(ex- girlfriend)Professional InformationProfessionProfessorWorkplaceRockland UniversityAppearancesFirstFirestarterLastThe Children's ParadeSeasons4-5Portrayed byJamie KennedyEli James (Jamie Kennedy) is a new character that was introduced in the first episode of season four. During a building fire on the Rockland University campus[1], he dies but is brought back, causing him to have a near death experience. This unlocks an ability that allows him to hear ghosts, but doesn't allow him to see them likeMelinda. At first he just believes he is losing his mind, but after hearing his deceased friend in ghost form he starts to accept his new ability. Throughout the fourth season, he adjusts to his newfound ability and starts helping Melinda cross ghosts over. Eli becomes a better person and a good friend to Melinda,JimandDelia. Even during Jim's phase as amnesiacSam Lucas, Eli was like a best friend to Jim, but his loyalty was to Melinda first which he made pretty clear to Jim when he wouldn't believe in Melinda's gift[2]. WhenRick Payneleaves for his sabatical, Eli pretty much steps in as the goofy expert that Melinda turns to except that his expertise is in psychology in which he has a PhD. His knowledge of the human mind becomes a very useful tool in helping to cross ghosts over into the light, something Melinda trusts him to do on his own. Eli becomes quite adept at his new side job helping ghosts.

characters.name: Ely Fisher
characters.description: "You two! You two! You think you're friends? Have a nice trip? You'd get justice, wouldn't you? You know why? 'Cause you're white! And you, you wouldn't get nothing! No justice for you, boy! No justice for you! Justice for what?." Ely Fisheris an African-American husband and father who was beaten to death by a racist white man and never received justice due to being portrayed as the one who started the fight. "I thought I heard his ribs crack."

characters.name: Gabriel Hastings
characters.description: Gabriel HastingsBiographical InformationAliasGabriel GordonGabriel LawrenceFamilial InformationParentsThomas Gordon(father)Professional InformationProfessionGhost WhispererAppearancesFirstThe CollectorLastStrangleholdCount6Seasons2,3Portrayed byIgnacio SerricchioGabriel Lawrenceis portrayed byIgnacio Serricchio, he was Melinda's alleged half-brother and like Melinda, he possesses the gift to talk to the dead too. Gabriel Hastings is introduced in Season 2, Episode 20, "The Collector" as Gabriel Lawrence. Melinda first sees Gabriel at a funeral home supposedly crossing over a tough looking biker. After tracking Gabriel down the two try and convince Amy Fields to go into the light but are unsuccessful. Later that day Gabriel visits Melinda and Jim at their home for dinner and drinks where he and Melinda discuss the changing spirit world. When he was a child, Gabriel knew he could interact with the dead, but nobody believed him, and he received psychiatric treatment in a mental health institution. Gabriel never had a living friend. In fact, all of his friends were dead, and he always hated watching them cross over into The Light. He managed to get out of the mental health institution by lying. He was in the same mental health institution where his mother also resided.

characters.name: Homer
characters.description: Homer is a ghost dog and appeared in the episode "Lost Boys."  He lived with Rat, Vic, Marty, and Ernie in the orphanage with them and died in the fire 50 years ago with the three of them except Ernie.  He stayed at the inn with them until Melinda got the 3 spirits to cross-over intothe Lightand then stayed withJim&Melindafor a while.  HomerBiographical InformationBorn?Died1956Cause of DeathFireAlias?Familial InformationOthers?AppearancesFirstLost BoysLastChildren of GhostsCount4Seasons1,2Seasons1,2

characters.name: Jim Clancy
characters.description: Jim ClancyAdultTeenBiographical InformationBorn1974DiedNovember 7, 2008 (aged 34)StatusDead (Occupying Sam Lucas' Body)Familial InformationMaritalMelinda Gordon(wife)ChildrenAiden Lucas Clancy(son)Unnamed Baby †(miscarried)SiblingsDaniel Clancy†(brother)ParentsFaith Clancy(mother)Aiden Clancy†(father)Professional InformationProfessionDoctor (while Sam Lucas)Paramedic (at time of death)WorkplaceHospitalAppearancesFirstPilotSeasons1-5Portrayed byDavid ConradTalon Smith (Young)Jim Clancywas the husband ofMelinda Gordon, the main character in the show. He worked as a paramedic and paramedic and firefighter and then a resident in season 5. Talon Smith (Young) Jim had blue eyes and brown hair. Jim was a generally accepting character, often talking and advising Mel. He was shown to love kids, often trying to convince Melinda to try to have them.  When she finally agrees and gives birth to Aiden Lucas, he becomes very protective of him.  He rejects Aiden being a medium, but learns to accept it eventually.

After Melinda felt that he was the one for her, she decided to tell him her secret, hoping that if she told him,However, in thesecond episodeof the second season, his name has been changed to Kyle.

characters.name: Marty Golden
characters.description: Marty Goldenwas a comedian fromGrandview, New York, who frequently performed at theComedy Cave. He datedSandra Hollowayuntil taking his own life. Marty Golden was an aspiring comedian who performed at theComedy CaveinGrandview. However, his comedic style did not appeal to the masses as much as his girlfriend,Sandra Holloway. When Sandra was offered a chance to go to Europe as part of a professional comedy tour, she refused, instead choosing to stay in Grandview to remain close to Marty. Marty tried to convince her to go, and after she continued to refuse, took his own life by jumping off a bridge. As a ghost, Marty was confused, not remembering taking his own life or even realizing that he was a ghost. He continued to visit the Comedy Cave untilAndrea Marinoand her friendMelinda Gordoncame to open mic night at the club. Marty appeared to Melinda, who helped him unravel the mystery of his death andcrossintothe light.

characters.name: Melinda Gordon
characters.actor_name: Jennifer Love HewittGrace Caroline Currey(young)Haley Pullo(young)
characters.description: Melinda GordonAdultYoung AdultTeenChildBiographical InformationBornMay 15, 1980Grandview, New York, USA(age 45)StatusAliveAliasMelMeloony Gordon (from her high school classmates)Familial InformationMaritalJim Clancy†(husband)Sam Lucas(husband)ChildrenAiden Lucas Clancy(son)SiblingsGabriel Hastings(stepbrother)ParentsBeth Gordon(mother)Paul Eastman†(father)Tom Gordon†(stepfather)OthersMary Ann Patterson†(grandmother)Julia Lee Lucas†(great-great grandmother)Tessa Lucas†(great-great-great grandmother)Professional InformationProfessionAntique shop ownerGhost whispererWorkplaceSame As It Never Was AntiquesAppearancesFirstPilotLastThe Children's ParadeCountAll EpisodesSeasons1-5Portrayed byJennifer Love HewittGrace Caroline Currey(young)Haley Pullo(young) Melinda Gordonis the owner of antique storeSame As It Never Was, who has the unique ability to talk to the deceased. She is the daughter ofBeth Gordonand the now-deceasedPaul Eastman. She was married toJim Clancyuntil his death; they had a son,Aiden Lucas. She later marriedSam Lucas. Melinda uses her ability to helpghostsby finding them peace by assisting them in any unfinished business, sometimes having to contact their living loved ones and tell them of her gift so that they can transfer messages to them just before they cross over into theLight. However, telling her secrets to the living often causes Melinda trouble, usually doubt and anger from the people she tries to help. Melinda is kind and always willing to help out others. She puts her relationships and friendships before herself and will do anything to keep her loved ones safe. She is naturally curious person and when helping earthbound spirits, she will go the distance in figuring out ways to help them, a lot of the time researching at all hours of the night to find out everything she can. Melinda can sometimes come off as angry when talking with her mother but she has always felt a deep hurt since her mother never tried to understand her gift. Her grandmother was her confidant and best friend and after she died, Melinda was very depressed because she felt like she had no one to talk to. She is a very independent person and worked very hard, with babysitting jobs as a teenager to afford her first car. She is seen to have saved her money to be able to open up her antique store. Sometimes she can been seen as scatter brained and very flaky, but she is always dealing with a ghost.

characters.name: Mitch
characters.description: Mitch is the brother of Andrea. He is a Real Estate mogul and loves his sister.

characters.name: Ned Banks
characters.description: Ned BanksBiographical InformationBornc. 1994 (age 31)StatusAliveFamilial InformationParentsDelia Banks(mother)Charlie Banks †(Father)AppearancesFirstLove Still Won't DieLastThe Children's ParadePortrayed byTyler Patrick Jones (Child)Christoph Sanders (Teenager)Ned Bankswas originally portrayed byTyler Patrick Jones, but starting with season 3 episode "Slam," Christoph Sanders took over the role. He is the son ofDeliaand Charlie Banks. The events of his adolescence have been part of the plots of some episodes. Ned is seen as a smart, sweet, young man. He can be disobedient to his mother, and sometimes abrupt towards her. In one episode, his mother says, "You study the Occult to help people." Ned replies rudely, "I study the occult because it fascinates me." Although he can be stiff and abrupt, he loves his mother unconditionally. He also respects and is even friends with Melinda. He sees Jim as a mentor and a father figure, since Ned's father passed. He is distraught when Jim 'dies'. He goes to the baskbetball court on the Thursday after Jim dies, like they always did, and Jim is seen as a ghost watching him. Ned is seen as a smart, sweet, young man. He can be disobedient to his mother, and sometimes abrupt towards her. In one episode, his mother says, "You study the Occult to help people." Ned replies rudely, "I study the occult because it fascinates me." Although he can be stiff and abrupt, he loves his mother unconditionally. He also respects and is even friends with Melinda. He sees Jim as a mentor and a father figure, since Ned's father passed. He is distraught when Jim 'dies'. He goes to the baskbetball court on the Thursday after Jim dies, like they always did, and Jim is seen as a ghost watching him. Ned isn't very popular, with a select group of odd friends. He treats women nicely, although he froze out one of his female friends in an episode. He is considerate, caring, and funny. Along with being a charmer, he is very gentle and sweet towards all people.

characters.name: Paul Eastman
characters.description: Paul EastmanBiographical InformationBorn1940sDied1993Cause of DeathMurdered by Tom GordonHit on the head with a piece of woodStatusDeceasedCrossed OverAliasPaulMasked ManFamilial InformationMaritalBeth Gordon(ex-fiance)ChildrenMelinda Gordon(daughter)GrandchildrenAiden Lucas(grandson)OthersJim Clancy(son-in-law)Professional InformationProfession?AppearancesFirstStrangleholdLastPater FamiliasCount3Seasons3Seasons3Portrayed byCorin NemecPaul Eastmanwas a ghost in season 3. InStranglehold (Part One), he is alluded to be a man who was sentenced for the murder of Michael Wilkins, a young boy who was strangled in the woods. Thomas Gordonwas the prosecuting ADA on the case.

characters.name: Professor Rick Payne
characters.description: Rick Paynebecomes a professor at Rockland University and marries his wife, Kate. He and his wife live somewhere in Grandview, at some point Kate Payne dies.

characters.name: Professor Rick Payne
characters.description: Rick Paynebecomes a professor at Rockland University and marries his wife, Kate. He and his wife live somewhere in Grandview, at some point Kate Payne dies.

characters.name: Rick Payne
characters.description: Rick PayneBiographical InformationStatusAliveFamilial InformationMaritalKate Payne †(wife)Professional InformationProfessionProfessor (Occult)WorkplaceRockland UniversityAppearancesFirstLove Never DiesLastFirestarterPortrayed byJay Mohr Payne usually wears a button down shirt with a tie, and jeans or khakis. He keeps his hair short and usually swept to the right. Payne likes to make jokes a lot. He is an amusing character throughout the series. He helpsMelindastudy things and people when she needs help with an earth-bound spirit. Although quite charming and funny, he tends to stay single. He keeps to himself a lot, spending most of his time in his office. He can be witty and often rude. He has a low amount of patience, but often helps Melinda without hesitation. He is a good friend of Melinda, but has a somewhat distant relationship withJim, implied later in the show that he might have had a romantic interest in Melinda. He is much kinder on the inside then he lets on. Payne likes to make jokes a lot. He is an amusing character throughout the series. He is much kinder on the inside then he lets on.

characters.name: Sam Lucas
characters.description: Sam LucasGhostSeason 4 (Possessed)Season 5Biographical InformationBornJuly 7, 1978DiedNovember 14, 2008 (aged 30)StatusDeceased (Body Occupied byJim Clancy)AliasJim ClancyFamilial InformationMaritalMelinda GordonProfessional InformationProfessionDoctorArchitectural Engineer(formerly) Sam Lucasis a character fromGhost Whisperer. Not much is known about Sam Lucas prior to his death, he had one sister named Jordan and two parents who were very controlling of Sam's life. He also had an ongoing off again on again relationship with a woman named Nicole. It is also known that Sam was an architect, but nothing else about his career or personal life.

characters.name: Sarah Evers
characters.description: "I was just like her. I was so scared. I was in such a dark place. I don't know how long I'd been in there. It seemed like forever." [...] "[...]That's where Roger kept me after he kidnapped me." Sarah Evers, first known as "Tammy Gardner" in the episodeBall & Chain, is a woman who disappeared and was last seen with "her husband" Roger Gardner. She returns as a confused spirit, searching for Roger and confused when she finds out Roger has another "wife". It's revealed, along with her real name, that she's actually a victim of Roger kidnapping her years ago from her family, brainwashing her into a name Roger picked and keeping her prisoner as a "wife", Evers developing Stockholm Syndrome as a result. When she broke out of her domination from remembering her family and tried to escape, Roger killed her and kidnapped another woman, whom Evers helped save. "What would happen to my family? I need to take care of them, of Roger."

characters.name: Sgt. Paul Adams
characters.description: Paul Adamswas ghost who went missing in Vietnam and came to askMelindafor help to  contact his son Michael.

characters.name: Shadows
characters.description: The Shadowsare non human dark forms that roam through Grandview first featured in Season 5. According toAiden, theShiniessaid that they are "made of parts of people that are left behind." This means that when a spirit goes into the light, sometimes only part(s) of it does and the other part(s) are left behind and become Shadows. In the beginning of Season 5, they can haunt a person and torture them with images of bad things that person has done in the past. This makes them go mad with guilt. In "Lost in the Shadows," Carl mentions that he is constantly seeing images of all the bad things he has done in his past.Melindafigures out that the Shadows have gotten to him. They can corrupt people and control what they do and say. Not only can they control people, they can also control ghosts. Their power of control and torture is threatened by Melinda and her son, Aiden. Since, they have the gift to crossover earthbound spirits into the light, the Shadows become weaker. The shadow also have the ability to make people feel colder and possibly ghosts too because ghost children call them "the cold things".

characters.name: Sharon Cho
characters.description: Sharon ChoSharon outsideTickle Me Pink SalonBiographical InformationStatusAliveFamilial InformationChildrenDylan Cho(son)Professional InformationProfessionHairdresserAppearancesFirstThe CrossingLastThe CrossingCount1Portrayed byJennifer TungSharonis a hairdresser, who works at theTickle Me Pink SaloninGrandview. Her son,Dylan, becomes friends with six-year-oldKenny Dale, a ghost. Sharon is increasingly exasperated with the mention of Kenny, whom she dismisses as a "make-believe friend" that Dylan is too old to have.
